items.title: 3 best picks in first round of 2026 NFL Draft
items.summary: We can’t help ourselves. The excitement overcomes us, and the possibilities are endless. Your favorite NFL team just added a talented player, and it’s difficult to ignore the upside. For that reason, here we are to judge the draft picks…

items.title: Texans fortify the trenches, taking Rutledge in Round 1 and McDonald in second round of NFL draft
items.summary: The Houston Texans addressed their biggest needs in this year’s NFL draft by shoring up their interior offensive and defensive lines with their top picks.  The Texans traded up two spots to take guard Keylan Rutledge with the 26th overall pick in the draft.  It marked the first time they’d taken a player in the first round since selecting quarterback C.J. Stroud and defensive end Will Anderson Jr. second and third overall in 2023.

items.title: Playoff-starved Jets take players with winning backgrounds to lead their NFL draft haul
items.summary: The New York Jets entered the NFL draft with plenty of roster holes and a culture under construction.  Texas Tech edge rusher David Bailey was the second overall pick, giving the pass rush an immediate boost.  The Jets took Oregon tight end Kenyon Sadiq 16th overall and then traded back into the first round to select Indiana wide receiver Omar Cooper Jr. at No. 30 — both playmakers for the offense.

items.title: A day later, Falcons cornerback duo of brothers Avieon and A.J. Terrell can't stop hugging
items.summary: Avieon Terrell and older brother A.J. shared an emotional moment during the NFL draft, thanks to the Atlanta Falcons.  The Falcons made Avieon Terrell, a cornerback from Clemson, their first pick in the draft on Friday.  A.J. Terrell, Atlanta's first-round pick in 2020, then shared a long hug with his brother at the family’s draft party.

After a trade-back with Pittsburgh in round 2 – No. 47 overall to No. 53 – the Indianapolis Colts found Zaire Franklin’s successor at middle linebacker with Georgia’s CJ Allen. In round 3, they invested the 78th overall pick in LSU safety A.J. Haulcy. [...]

items.title: Sauce Gardner: I’m Colts’ first-round pick (next year, too)
items.summary: INDIANAPOLIS – Sauce Gardner isn’t buying into the notion that the Indianapolis Colts don’t have a pick in the first round of Thursday night’s NFL Draft. “I’m the first-round pick,’’ he said this week with a smile. Actually, more than that. “I’m the first-round pick two times,’’ Gardner said. This year, and in 2027. That [...]

items.title: Colts announce '2026 Schedule Challenge'
items.summary: The Indianapolis Colts today announced the “2026 Schedule Challenge,” where one contestant can win two tickets to Super Bowl LXI by most accurately predicting the Colts’ entire 18-week schedule for the 2026 regular season.
